content.full_text:
APRIL 17, 1990

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" ST., SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CA.

TO WHOM IT MAY CONCERN:

I AM WRITING TO THE COMMISSION REGARDING REPORTING OR REGISTRATION REQUIREMENTS FOR LOBBYISTS EMPLOYED BY TRADE UNIONS. HERE IN SACRAMENTO AT LOCAL 12, AMERICAN FEDERATION OF MUSICIANS, WE HAVE A "LEGISLATIVE LIAISON OFFICER" APPOINTED BY THE PRESIDENT. WHILE THE OFFICER'S DUTIES ARE LARGELY UNDEFINED, HE IS COMPENSATED FOR HIS WORK BY MEANS OF A MEMBERSHIP DUES EXEMPTION. TO MY KNOWLEDGE THE OFFICER HAS NOT CONTACTED ANY ELECTED OFFICIUS REGARDING LEGISLATION OR OTHER MATTERS. HE DOES REPORT, PERIODICALLY, TO THE LOCAL BOARD OF DIRECTORS REGARDING LEGISLATIVE COMMITTEE MEETINGS AND HATS ORGANIZATION MEETINGS WHICH HE HAS ATTENDED. IN ORDER TO AVOID LEGAL COMPLICATIONS WHICH MIGHT RESULT FROM HIS ACTIVITIES, SHOULD HE REGISTER AS A PAID LOBBYIST WITH THE APPROPRIATE AUTHORITIES?

THANK YOU.

SINCERELY,

[signature]
JOHN P. LIVI
4529 CAHUENGA.
FAIR OAKS, CA. 95628

[illegible] [illegible]

[illegible] ASSISTING COUNSEL ATTORNEY AND

AND ASSERTION WILL ARE FOR A PART [illegible] IN BRIEFINGS

AS B[illegible] BRIEF A IN DISTINQUISH [illegible] 2 POINTS IS TO

CIRCUIT DISMISS OPINION OF COURT [illegible] SHALL BE IN

OPINION ADDITION POINTS AND A BRIEF [illegible] AND

WILL B [illegible] WHEN ORAL [illegible] SHALL BE

IN OPIN IS (B) I BASIC AS OPIN IS ORALLY

sections.question: I am writing to the Commission regarding reporting or registration requirements for lobbyists employed by trade unions. Here in Sacramento at Local 12, American Federation of Musicians, we have a "legislative liaison officer" appointed by the president. While the officer's duties are largely undefined, he is compensated for his work by means of a membership dues exemption. To my knowledge, this officer has not contacted any elected officials regarding legislation or other matters. He does report, periodically, to the local board of directors regarding legislative committee meetings and arts organization meetings which he has attended. In order to avoid legal complications which might result from his activities, should he register as a paid lobbyist with the appropriate authorities?
sections.question_synthetic: Does a union's legislative liaison officer who is compensated through a membership dues exemption and reports on legislative committee and arts organization meetings need to register as a paid lobbyist?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. The document contains only the question posed by the requester. No CONCLUSION or FPPC response section is present in this document. The handwritten portion at the bottom is illegible and contains no substantive content.

While the officer's duties are largely undefined, he is compensated for his work by means of a membership dues exemption. To my knowledge, this officer has not contacted any elected officials regarding legislation or other matters. He does report, periodically, to the local board of directors regarding legislative committee meetings and arts organization meetings which he has attended. In order to avoid legal complications which might result from his activities, should he register as a paid lobbyist with the appropriate authorities?
embedding.first_500_words: April 17, 1990 Fair Political Practices Commission 428 "J" St., Suite 800 Sacramento, CA. To Whom It May Concern: I am writing to the Commission regarding reporting or registration requirements for lobbyists employed by trade unions. Here in Sacramento at Local 12, American Federation of Musicians, we have a "legislative liaison officer" appointed by the president. While the officer's duties are largely undefined, he is compensated for his work by means of a membership dues exemption. To my knowledge, this officer has not contacted any elected officials regarding legislation or other matters. He does report, periodically, to the local board of directors regarding legislative committee meetings and arts organization meetings which he has attended. In order to avoid legal complications which might result from his activities, should he register as a paid lobbyist with the appropriate authorities? Thank you. Sincerely, John P. Kivi John P. Kivi 4529 Cahueengog Fair Oaks, CA. 95628 The image appears to be a handwritten note or letter, but the handwriting is extremely faint and mostly illegible. Only a few scattered words and numbers are partially visible, such as "John," "2528," and some numbers like "4" and "6." Due to the quality and faintness of the handwriting, it is not possible to transcribe or provide a clear summary of the content. If you have another scan with better quality, please provide it for better results.
embedding.summary: John P. Kivi from Local 12, American Federation of Musicians, writes to the FPPC seeking guidance on whether their legislative liaison officer, who is compensated through membership dues exemption but has not directly contacted elected officials, is required to register as a paid lobbyist.
